title: Breach Discovery — Time-to-Detect from First PHI Indicator

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
`notable` HIPAA_Breach_Candidate=1 earliest=-90d
| eval detect_time=_time
| join IncidentId type=left [
    search index=dlp sourcetype="o365:dlp" earliest=-90d
    | eval first_indicator=strptime(CreatedTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    | stats min(first_indicator) as first_dlp by IncidentId ]
| eval hours_to_detect=round((detect_time-first_dlp)/3600,2)
| table IncidentId, first_dlp, detect_time, hours_to_detect, rule_name, status
| sort - hours_to_detect
value: Measures elapsed time between earliest technical indicator of a potential PHI incident and formal incident creation so discovery timelines support Breach Notification Rule evidence.
implementation: (1) Tag correlation searches that create HIPAA candidate notables; (2) normalize UTC; (3) executive dashboard for legal discovery SLA.
visualization: Histogram (hours_to_detect), Table (incidents), Single value (median detect hours).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
